text.title: video-generation-provider-selection Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for video-generation-provider-selection. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Choose Runway Gen-3 when the task requires a reliable production API, video-to-video transformations, lip-sync for talking head videos, or precise motion control.
rule.rationale: Runway is the only provider with a stable public REST API that supports all four of these capabilities simultaneously as of 2026.

rule.statement: Choose Luma Dream Machine when the task requires simple text-to-video workflow, image animation, video extension capabilities, or is budget-conscious.
rule.rationale: Luma's credits-based pricing is typically lower per-second than Runway; it supports video extension which Sora 2 does not.

rule.statement: Choose Sora 2 when highest quality is the priority, long-form content up to 60 seconds is needed, photorealistic output is required, and a ChatGPT Pro subscription is available.
rule.rationale: Sora 2 produces the best character consistency and supports 60-second single-call generation; however, API access is gated to ChatGPT Pro/Plus subscribers as of 2026.

rule.statement: Choose Veo 3 when 4K resolution is required, native audio or dialogue is needed, Google Cloud Vertex AI infrastructure is available, or the use case is enterprise-scale.
rule.rationale: Veo 3 is the only provider with native audio generation; 4K output via Vertex AI is not available as a self-service API to most developers as of 2026.

rule.statement: Choose Replicate when open-source models are needed, custom model fine-tuning is required, cost optimization via self-hosting is planned, or the workflow is experimental.
rule.rationale: Replicate provides access to 50+ open video models (CogVideoX, AnimateDiff, Zeroscope, Stable Video Diffusion) with per-generation pricing and the option to self-host.
